title: MB to GB Converter
shortDescription: Convert MB to GB using the binary 1024 ratio for file sizes and storage values.
metaDescription: Convert megabytes to gigabytes using the 1024-based storage ratio for file sizes, disk space, and backup planning.
intro: Enter a megabyte value to convert it to gigabytes using 1 GB = 1024 MB.
overview:
This converter is built for storage measurements that follow the binary scale, where gigabytes are counted as 1024 megabytes. That makes it useful when you are comparing file sizes, checking drive space, or reading system storage figures.

The result shows the gigabyte value directly from the MB amount you enter, so you can move between units without doing the division yourself. If you need the reverse direction, use the matching GB to MB converter to go back from gigabytes to megabytes.
howItWorks: Type the MB amount you want to convert.
The tool applies the binary 1024 ratio: 1 GB = 1024 MB.
Read the GB result and switch to a reverse conversion if needed.

useCases.title: Archive size before upload
useCases.description: Check whether a 768 MB export will fit within a 1 GB storage limit before sending or archiving it.

useCases.title: Backup folder planning
useCases.description: Convert backup totals from MB into GB when estimating how much space a full folder sync will require.

useCases.title: Storage panel comparison
useCases.description: Match a drive or partition figure shown in megabytes to the same value in gigabytes for easier reading.

examples.title: Media file size

examples.title: Software installer
examples.description: Useful when a download page lists the installer size in MB.

examples.title: Partial storage quota
examples.description: Shows the exact binary conversion for a smaller quota or cache folder.

faq.question: Does this use decimal or binary gigabytes?
faq.answer: It uses the binary scale: 1 GB equals 1024 MB. That is the right interpretation for many storage displays and file-size calculations.

faq.question: Why does 500 MB not become exactly 0.5 GB?
faq.answer: Because 0.5 GB equals 512 MB on the binary scale. A 500 MB value is slightly below half a gigabyte.

faq.question: How do I read the result for very small MB values?
faq.answer: Small inputs may produce long decimal GB values. That is normal because the tool keeps the binary ratio exact instead of rounding too early.

faq.question: What mistake should I avoid when comparing storage labels?
faq.answer: Do not mix this binary conversion with decimal marketing values that treat 1 GB as 1000 MB. The meaning changes the result.
